title: The Northern Teaching Country

features.name: the kilometer stone
features.desc-on-touch: You read a marker someone has worn smooth walking; it knows every traveler by their footfall pattern, and now it teaches each new arrival.

features.name: the teaching shelter
features.desc-on-touch: A simple house where walkers stop and find left behind: maps, notes, advice written by those who came before and learned to pass it on.

features.name: the marked paddock
features.desc-on-touch: A field where someone has walked the perimeter until it wore a path; the land learned what the walker was teaching.

features.name: the second footstep gate
features.desc-on-touch: A fence-gate worn by walkers who came before. Each person who passes through it becomes the teacher of the next arrival.

features.name: the memory worn into stone
features.desc-on-touch: A place in the road where the earth has recorded everyone who walked it; you read the path and understand you are part of what it is teaching.

inscription: the northern road learns to teach itself by remembering every walker who came before you

reverie.title: REVERIE — the road teaching itself northward
narr: You walk the northern way and feel the ground remember.
Leif beside you, tracing kilometer markers worn smooth
by walkers no one knows. The road itself is teaching—
not through signs, but through the shape of where feet have gone.
He says: "It already knows you're here." You believe him.
The path ahead curves the way ten thousand arrivals curved it.

file.name: KilometerMarkers_NorthernRoute_Annotations.txt
file.note: Walking journals and notes left in shelters; each entry teaches the next walker what the first arrivals learned.

exits.label: back south along the memory-worn road
further north where teaching becomes the only language
westward to paddocks where the teaching grows in parallel
